# Indian Data Project

> India publishes vast amounts of government data — budget documents, economic surveys, RBI reports, census figures, crime statistics, election results. But this data is buried in dense PDFs, broken APIs, and spreadsheets formatted for bureaucrats. Indian Data Project makes it accessible, visual, and engaging for regular citizens. 11 data domains as scrollable visual stories, 161 searchable citizen questions, 3 personalisation calculators, 80 open JSON endpoints. Open source (AGPL), no login, no paywall.

## Why this exists

India's government data infrastructure has a fundamental accessibility problem. Data is scattered across dozens of government websites. PDFs with hundreds of pages and no search. Excel files with merged cells and no column headers. APIs with documentation from 2014 that reference endpoints that no longer exist. Data portals with datasets that haven't been updated since 2020.

The data exists. It's just formatted for bureaucrats and statisticians, not for the 1.4 billion citizens it belongs to. A citizen shouldn't need two hours and five government portals to answer a simple question about their own country.

Indian Data Project takes data from RBI, MOSPI, World Bank, NCRB, Election Commission of India, UDISE+, NFHS-5, and 30+ other government and international statistical agencies, and presents it as interactive visual stories with plain-language annotations. The goal is making Indian public data genuinely useful for society — whether you're a student trying to understand the economy, a journalist sourcing a story, a teacher building a lesson plan, or a citizen who wants to know how their state compares to others.

## What you can do here

**Explore 11 data domains as visual stories.** Budget, Economy, RBI monetary policy, State Finances, Census & Demographics, Education, Employment, Healthcare, Environment, Elections, Crime & Safety. Each domain has a scrollable narrative with interactive charts and annotations that explain what the numbers mean in plain language. Every chart is shareable, downloadable, and embeddable.

**Ask questions in plain language.** The search indexes 161 citizen questions like "Why are prices rising?", "How safe is my city?", "Is my state richer or poorer than average?", and "How much does India spend on healthcare per citizen?" — each mapped to the specific data section that answers it.

**Personalise the data to your life.** Three calculators: see how RBI rate changes affect your EMI, check how much your purchasing power has changed since any year from 2014, and pull up a report card for your state comparing it against all others across 28 metrics.

**Use the data for your work.** Journalists get 76 embeddable charts, 7 pre-assembled story kits with narrative angles, and an embed builder. Teachers get lesson plans mapped to NCERT Class 10-12 curriculum with classroom mode (larger fonts). Developers get 80 JSON endpoints with no API key required.

## What makes this credible

Every number traces to an authoritative primary source. No data is invented, approximated, or editorially adjusted. Methodology pages for each domain document exactly where each number comes from, how it was processed, and what the limitations are. The code is open source (AGPL) and the data files are freely downloadable as JSON.

11 automated data pipelines keep the portal current. When RBI publishes new monetary policy figures, when MOSPI releases updated CPI data, when World Bank refreshes its India indicators — the pipelines fetch, validate, and update without manual intervention.

## Who built this and why

This is an independent civic project built by one person, not affiliated with or funded by any government body. It was born from frustration: India's digital infrastructure at the identity layer (Aadhaar) and payments layer (UPI) is world-class, but the data transparency layer — how citizens access and understand public information about their own country — is decades behind. This portal is an attempt to close that gap for the benefit of Indian society.

## Eleven data domains

### Hub
- / — Visual portal showcasing all data domains with live stats from each dataset

### Open Data
- /open-data — Direct access to 80 JSON endpoints with source links, schema notes, and no API key required.

### For Journalists
- /for-journalists — Newsroom toolkit landing page for fast chart sourcing, attribution-ready stats, and publishing workflows.
- /for-journalists/gallery — Curated chart gallery with ready-to-use visuals, captions, and source context.
- /for-journalists/story-kits — Pre-assembled story kits with chart bundles, narrative angles, and cited evidence.
- /for-journalists/embed-builder — No-code embed builder to generate responsive iframe snippets with attribution.

### For Teachers
- /for-teachers — Educator hub for classroom-ready data activities and curriculum-linked resources.
- /for-teachers/lesson-plans — NCERT-mapped lesson plans with learning goals, prompts, and dataset-backed exercises.

### Contribute
- /contribute — Contributor guide for issues, data corrections, translations, and open-source collaboration.

### Budget Domain
- /budget — Scrollytelling narrative (9 sections): animated headline, revenue waffle chart, deficit rupee bar, 20-year budget trends (expenditure vs receipts + deficit % GDP), budget vs actual deviation chart (10 ministries), expenditure treemap, Sankey money flow, state choropleth, per-capita breakdown
- /budget/explore — Sortable ministry-wise expenditure table with expandable scheme detail. CSV export available.
- /budget/calculator — Personal tax calculator (FY 2025-26). Enter income, choose Old/New regime with deductions support (80C, 80D, HRA, 24b, NPS, 80TTA), see slab-by-slab tax breakdown and ministry-wise allocation of your tax. Shareable image card.
- /budget/methodology — Data sources, tax computation methodology, formatting conventions, limitations.
- /budget/glossary — Plain-language definitions of budget terms: fiscal deficit, revenue deficit, capital expenditure, cess, surcharge, devolution, FRBM, GDP, crore/lakh.

### Economy Domain
- /economy — Scrollytelling visual breakdown: GDP growth trend (line chart), inflation tracking (CPI/WPI line chart), trade balance (area chart), fiscal position, sectoral composition (horizontal bar chart with annotations)
- /economy/explore — Searchable table of all economic indicators with year-over-year comparisons
- /economy/methodology — Data sources, indicator definitions, survey methodology, limitations
- /economy/glossary — Plain-language definitions of economic terms: GDP, inflation, CPI, WPI, trade deficit, current account, FDI, sectoral composition, base year, advance estimates.

### RBI Data Domain
- /rbi — Scrollytelling narrative: repo rate hero, monetary policy trajectory (line chart), inflation targeting vs 4% mandate (line chart with band), M3 money supply growth, credit expansion (two-series line chart), forex reserves (area chart), exchange rate INR/USD
- /rbi/explore — Browseable indicator explorer with categories: monetary, liquidity, credit, external. Select any indicator to see full time series.
- /rbi/methodology — Data sources (RBI DBIE, World Bank, MPC press releases), indicator definitions, WB codes, data freshness, limitations
- /rbi/glossary — Plain-language definitions of RBI terms: repo rate, CRR, SLR, MPC, monetary policy stance, inflation targeting, M3 money supply, forex reserves, basis points.

### State Finances Domain
- /states — Scrollytelling narrative: state GSDP landscape (horizontal bar chart), growth leaders, revenue self-sufficiency (stacked bars showing own tax vs central transfers), fiscal health (debt-to-GSDP with FRBM 3% reference line), per capita GSDP
- /states/explore — Browseable indicator explorer with categories: GSDP, Revenue, Fiscal, Per Capita. Select any indicator to compare across 31 of 36 states/UTs (5 small UTs have incomplete accounts).
- /states/methodology — Data sources (RBI Handbook, State Finances report, Finance Commission), indicator definitions, data freshness (FY 2022-23 vintage), limitations
- /states/glossary — Plain-language definitions of state finance terms: GSDP, per capita income, own tax revenue, central transfers, devolution, FRBM Act, fiscal deficit, debt-to-GSDP, Finance Commission, self-sufficiency ratio.

### Census & Demographics Domain
- /census — Scrollytelling narrative: population growth (1.45 billion), age structure waffle chart (young/working/elderly), vital statistics (birth rate, death rate, fertility rate, life expectancy), infant mortality with state breakdown, literacy gender gap butterfly chart, urbanization trends
- /census/explore — Browseable indicator explorer with categories: Population, Demographics, Literacy, Health. Select any indicator to compare across states.
- /census/methodology — Data sources (Census of India 2011, World Bank API, NFHS-5, SRS 2023, NPC Projections), multi-vintage data strategy, indicator definitions, limitations
- /census/glossary — Plain-language definitions of demographic terms: census, decadal growth, sex ratio, dependency ratio, infant mortality rate, total fertility rate, literacy rate, urbanization, NFHS, SRS.

### Education Domain
- /education — Scrollytelling narrative: enrollment triumph (primary/secondary/tertiary GER trends), gender convergence in enrollment, dropout cliff (primary 1.9% → secondary 14.1%), learning quality gap (ASER outcomes), teacher challenge (pupil-teacher ratios), education spending vs global peers
- /education/explore — Browseable indicator explorer with categories: enrollment, quality, infrastructure, spending. Select any indicator to compare across 32 states/UTs.
- /education/methodology — Data sources (UDISE+ 2023-24, ASER 2024, World Bank), indicator definitions, limitations
- /education/glossary — Plain-language definitions of education terms: GER, NER, dropout rate, PTR, ASER, UDISE, NEP 2020, foundational literacy, RTE Act, and more.

### Employment Domain
- /employment — Scrollytelling narrative: participation puzzle (LFPR trends), structural shift (agriculture declining, services rising), youth unemployment crisis (3-4x overall rate), gender gap in LFPR, informality challenge (self-employment dominance), rural vs urban employment patterns
- /employment/explore — Browseable indicator explorer with categories: unemployment, participation, sectoral, informality. Select any indicator to compare across states.
- /employment/methodology — Data sources (PLFS 2023-24, World Bank, RBI KLEMS), indicator definitions, limitations
- /employment/glossary — Plain-language definitions of employment terms: LFPR, unemployment rate, PLFS, informal sector, gig economy, structural transformation, and more.

### Healthcare Domain
- /healthcare — Scrollytelling narrative: infrastructure deficit (0.5 hospital beds per 1,000 vs WHO 3.5), health spending (3.3% GDP), out-of-pocket burden (48% of health spending), immunization coverage, disease burden (TB, NCDs), doctor gap across states
- /healthcare/explore — Browseable indicator explorer with categories: infrastructure, spending, immunization, disease. Select any indicator to compare across states.
- /healthcare/methodology — Data sources (NHP 2022, NFHS-5, World Bank), indicator definitions, limitations
- /healthcare/glossary — Plain-language definitions of healthcare terms: PHC, CHC, out-of-pocket spending, immunization, TB incidence, hospital beds per 1000, and more.

### Environment Domain
- /environment — Scrollytelling narrative (5 sections): PM2.5 air quality trends and city AQI rankings, forest cover from ISFR 2023, installed electricity capacity mix (coal to solar transition 2015-2024), CO₂ per capita with global peers, groundwater stress and reservoir storage
- /environment/explore — Browseable indicator explorer with categories: air, forest, water. Select any indicator to compare across states.
- /environment/methodology — Data sources (CPCB, ISFR 2023, CEA, CWC, CGWB, World Bank), indicator definitions, limitations
- /environment/glossary — Plain-language definitions of environment terms: AQI, PM2.5, NAAQS, forest cover, groundwater stress, renewable energy, CO₂ emissions, and more.

### Elections Domain
- /elections — Scrollytelling narrative (5 sections): voter turnout trends 1957-2024 with event annotations, party landscape evolution (stacked area chart of 8 party groups across 17 elections), Lok Sabha 2024 verdict (Parliament hemicycle visualization of 543 seats), money & muscle (criminal cases, assets, education of MPs from ADR data), women in Parliament (trend with 33% reservation target line)
- /elections/explore — Browseable indicator explorer with state-level election data (voter turnout 2024 by state)
- /elections/methodology — Data sources (ECI, TCPD Lok Dhaba, ADR/MyNeta, Lok Sabha Secretariat), party grouping methodology, data vintage, limitations
- /elections/glossary — Plain-language definitions of election terms: Lok Sabha, FPTP, constituency, EVM, NOTA, ECI, MCC, affidavit, ADR, delimitation, coalition, Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam.

### Crime & Safety Domain
- /crime — Scrollytelling narrative (6 sections): crime overview (IPC/SLL split, national trend, state choropleth), crimes against women (4.45 lakh cases, cruelty and kidnapping lead), road accidents (1.68 lakh killed, 461 daily, overspeeding 69%), cybercrime (3x growth, 3% FIR conversion), police infrastructure (151 per lakh vs UN 222, 11.7% women), justice pipeline (funnel from FIR to conviction, 39.1% rate, 3.5 year avg trial)
- /crime/explore — State-wise crime indicators: overall rates, women crime rates, road fatalities, police-population ratio, vacancies
- /crime/methodology — NCRB methodology (FIR-based, cognizable only), underreporting caveats, Kerala paradox, MoRTH road data, World Bank homicide data, 2022 data vintage
- /crime/glossary — Plain-language definitions: FIR, chargesheet, cognizable offence, IPC, SLL, conviction rate, cybercrime, and more.

### Cross-Domain Topics
- /topics — Hub page with 12 curated cross-domain topic cards. Each topic weaves data from 2-5 domains into a narrative view.
- /topics/women-in-india — Women in India: political voice (women MPs 1952-2024), economic participation (female LFPR), education & health gaps, safety & violence (4.45L crimes against women). Domains: Elections, Employment, Education, Census, Healthcare, Crime.
- /topics/fiscal-health — India's Fiscal Health: GDP growth engine, spending vs borrowing (fiscal deficit % GDP), RBI monetary policy lever. Domains: Budget, Economy, RBI, States.
- /topics/inflation-cost — Inflation & Cost of Living: CPI price trajectory, RBI rate response, fiscal connection. Domains: Economy, RBI, Budget.
- /topics/education-employment — Education to Employment Pipeline: enrollment scale (secondary GER), jobs gap (unemployment rate), education spending. Domains: Education, Employment, Census, Budget.
- /topics/health-outcomes — Health Outcomes: child health (IMR trends), infrastructure gap (hospital beds, doctors), health spending (% GDP). Domains: Healthcare, Census, Budget, States.
- /topics/regional-inequality — Regional Inequality: income divide (per capita GSDP by state), education disparity, fiscal transfer mechanisms. Domains: States, Budget, Census, Education, Healthcare.
- /topics/climate-energy — Climate & Energy Transition: energy mix (renewables growth), carbon footprint, forest cover by state. Domains: Environment, Economy, Budget.
- /topics/youth-jobs — Youth & Jobs: youth unemployment (3x national rate), structural shift (sectoral employment), quality of work. Domains: Employment, Education, Census, Economy.
- /topics/urban-rural — Urban vs Rural Divide: urbanization wave (population growth rate), access gap (healthcare, literacy). Domains: Census, Employment, Healthcare, Environment, Education.
- /topics/democratic-health — India's Democratic Health: turnout arc (1952-2024), quality of representation (criminal cases, women MPs, assets). Domains: Elections, Census, Education.
- /topics/agriculture-food — Agriculture & Food Security: productivity paradox (42% workforce, 15% GDP), food-inflation link, sustainability pressures. Domains: Budget, Economy, Employment, Environment.
- /topics/water-crisis — Water Crisis: groundwater stress by state, depletion facts, water-health connection. Domains: Environment, Census, Healthcare.

### Personalization Calculators
- /rbi/calculator — EMI Impact Calculator: See how repo rate changes affect your monthly EMI. Choose home/car/personal loan, set amount and tenure, view rate impact at ±25/50 bps scenarios. Uses live repo rate (currently 5.25%) and bank spreads (SBI EBLR).
- /economy/calculator — Cost-of-Living Calculator: Input your monthly expenses by category (rent, groceries, transport, education, healthcare, utilities). Compare against any year from 2014+. Uses category-specific CPI by COICOP division (food, housing, health, transport, education) where available, headline CPI as fallback.
- /states/your-state — State Report Card: Select any Indian state to see performance across 6 domains (Economy, Budget, Census, Education, Employment, Healthcare). ~25 metrics with ranks, quartile badges (top/middle/bottom 25%), and national average comparison.

## Key data points

### Budget (Budget Estimate 2025-26)
- Total expenditure: Rs 50,65,345 crore
- Revenue receipts: Rs 34,20,905 crore
- Capital expenditure: Rs 11,21,059 crore
- Fiscal deficit target: 4.4% of GDP
- Population estimate used: 145 crore (1.45 billion)

### Economic Survey (2025-26)
- Real GDP growth: 6.5% (2024-25 Provisional Estimate)
- Projected growth: 7.4% (2025-26 NSO First Advance Estimate, Jan 2026)
- CPI inflation: 4.0% (Economic Survey revised projection)
- Nominal GDP: Rs 3,56,98,000 crore
- Current account deficit: -0.8% of GDP (H1 FY2025-26)
- Per capita GDP: Rs 2,44,507

### RBI Data (2025-26)
- Repo rate: 5.25% (Neutral stance, as of February 6, 2026)
- CRR: 3.00%, SLR: 18.00%
- CPI inflation (World Bank latest): 4.95%
- Forex reserves: $643 billion (World Bank latest; RBI weekly: $724B as of Feb 2026)
- Broad money (M3) growth: 11.3%
- Exchange rate: ~83.67 INR per USD (FY2024-25 annual average)
- 30 repo rate decisions tracked from 2014 onward

### State Finances (FY 2022-23)
- Top GSDP: Maharashtra Rs 35.28 lakh crore
- National combined state GDP: Rs 280.73 lakh crore
- Growth range: 2.9% to 25.4% (constant prices)
- Data across 31 of 36 states/UTs (5 small UTs have incomplete accounts)
- Average per capita GSDP: Rs 1,71,687
- Data source: RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian States

### Census & Demographics
- Total population: ~1.45 billion (World Bank 2024 estimate)
- Population growth rate: ~0.8% (decelerating from 1.6% in 2001)
- Literacy rate: 74.04% (Census 2011; male 82.14%, female 65.46%)
- Sex ratio: 943 females per 1000 males (Census 2011)
- Total fertility rate: 2.0 (NFHS-5 2019-21, below replacement level of 2.1)
- Infant mortality rate: 28 per 1000 live births (SRS 2023)
- Life expectancy: 70.19 years (World Bank 2023)
- Urbanization: ~36% (World Bank 2023)
- Data sources: Census of India 2011, World Bank Open Data, NFHS-5, SRS 2023, National Population Commission

### Education
- Total students: 248.0 million (UDISE+ 2023-24, Pre-Primary to Higher Secondary)
- Total schools: 1.47 million (14.72 lakh)
- GER Primary: 93.0%, Secondary: 77.4%, Tertiary: 28.4% (GER methodology changed in UDISE+ 2022-23 with SDMIS)
- Pupil-teacher ratio: 25 (national average)
- Education spending: 4.1% of GDP (NEP 2020 target: 6%)
- Data sources: UDISE+ 2023-24, ASER 2024, World Bank Open Data

### Employment
- Unemployment rate: 4.2% (PLFS Oct-Dec 2025)
- Labour force participation rate: 55.8%
- Female LFPR: 34.9% (CWS), Youth unemployment: 10.2% (PLFS 2023-24 usual status, 15-29 age group)
- Agricultural employment: declining from 60% (2000) to 42% (2023)
- Services employment: rising from 23% (2000) to 32% (2023)
- Data sources: PLFS Quarterly Bulletin, PLFS Annual Report 2023-24, World Bank, RBI KLEMS

### Healthcare
- Hospital beds: 0.5 per 1,000 (WHO recommends 3.5)
- Physicians: 0.7 per 1,000
- Health expenditure: 3.3% of GDP (global average ~6%)
- Out-of-pocket spending: 45.1% of total health expenditure (World Bank 2021, declining from 62% in 2014)
- DPT immunization: 91%, Measles: 92%
- TB incidence: 199 per 100,000 (highest global burden)
- Data sources: NHP 2022 (CBHI), NFHS-5, World Bank Open Data

### Crime & Safety (NCRB 2022)
- Total cognizable crimes 2022: 58,24,946
- Crime rate: 422.2 per lakh population
- Crimes against women: 4,45,256 (rate: 66.4 per lakh women)
- Road accident deaths: 1,68,491 (461 per day)
- Conviction rate: 39.1%
- Chargesheet rate: 74.6%
- Cybercrime FIRs: 65,893 (I4C complaints: 22.68 lakh)
- Police-population ratio: 151.1 per lakh (UN recommended: 222)
- Women in police: 11.7% (2,44,961)
- Average trial duration: 3.5 years
- Data sources: NCRB "Crime in India" 2022, MoRTH, BPRD, I4C, World Bank

### Elections (Lok Sabha 1957-2024)
- Total electors 2024: 96.88 crore (968.8 million)
- Voter turnout 2024: 65.8% (17th Lok Sabha)
- Highest ever turnout: 67.5% (2014)
- BJP seats 2024: 240 (36.6% vote share), INC seats: 99 (21.2%)
- NDA total: 293 seats, INDIA bloc: 234 seats
- MPs with criminal cases: 46% (251 out of 543), Serious charges: 31%
- Average MP assets: Rs 46.34 crore (ADR analysis of affidavits)
- Women MPs: 74 out of 543 (13.6%), Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam targets 33%
- Data sources: Election Commission of India, TCPD Lok Dhaba (Ashoka University), ADR/MyNeta.info, Lok Sabha Secretariat

## Data sources

- Open Budgets India (openbudgetsindia.org) — structured budget data
- Union Budget documents (indiabudget.gov.in) — Expenditure Budget Vol. 1 & 2, Receipt Budget, Finance Bill
- Economic Survey of India (indiabudget.gov.in/economicsurvey/) — annual economic review
- Census projections and RBI reports for population estimates
- RBI DBIE (data.rbi.org.in) — Database on Indian Economy
- RBI Monetary Policy Statements — repo rate decisions, stance changes
- World Bank Open Data API — monetary, liquidity, credit, and external indicators
- RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian States — state-level GSDP, per capita, growth rates
- RBI State Finances: A Study of Budgets — revenue composition, fiscal deficit, debt
- Finance Commission of India — tax devolution formulas, grants
- Census of India (censusindia.gov.in) — decennial census 2011 baseline population, literacy, urbanization
- National Family Health Survey (NFHS-5, 2019-21) — TFR, IMR, stunting, wasting, immunization by state
- Sample Registration System (SRS 2023) — birth rate, death rate, IMR annual estimates
- National Population Commission — population projections 2011-2036
- UDISE+ Flash Statistics 2023-24 (udiseplus.gov.in) — school enrollment, infrastructure, teacher data
- ASER 2024 (asercentre.org) — learning outcomes and foundational skills assessment
- PLFS Quarterly Bulletin (mospi.gov.in) — unemployment, LFPR, worker population ratio
- PLFS Annual Report 2023-24 — state-wise employment indicators
- RBI KLEMS Database — sectoral employment and output data
- National Health Profile 2022 (cbhidghs.mohfw.gov.in) — hospital infrastructure, PHCs, CHCs, doctors
- NFHS-5 (rchiips.org/nfhs) — immunization coverage, health indicators by state
- MOSPI eSankhyiki CPI API (api.mospi.gov.in/api/cpi/getCPIIndex) — group-wise monthly CPI indices and YoY inflation rates, Base 2012=100. Primary source for CPI by COICOP division (FY 2019+)
- IMF CPI dataset via DBnomics (db.nomics.world/IMF/CPI) — historical CPI by COICOP division (Food, Housing, Health, Transport, Education) for India, CY 2014-2018
- SBI/HDFC/ICICI published rate cards — loan spreads over repo rate (home, car, personal loans)
- Election Commission of India (eci.gov.in) — constituency results, turnout data, election schedule
- TCPD Lok Dhaba (lokdhaba.ashoka.edu.in) — Trivedi Centre for Political Data, Ashoka University — historical Lok Sabha results 1962-2024
- ADR / MyNeta (adrindia.org, myneta.info) — Association for Democratic Reforms — MP criminal records, assets, education from affidavits
- Lok Sabha Secretariat — Women MPs count per election, parliamentary composition data
- NCRB "Crime in India" (ncrb.gov.in) — cognizable crime statistics by IPC/SLL, crimes against women, state-wise breakdowns
- MoRTH Road Transport & Highways Annual Report — road accidents, causes, fatalities by state
- Bureau of Police Research & Development (BPRD) — police strength, vacancies, infrastructure
- Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre (I4C) — cybercrime complaints and financial loss estimates
- World Bank — intentional homicide rate for international comparison

## Downloadable datasets (JSON)

### Budget
- /data/budget/2025-26/summary.json — Budget headline numbers
- /data/budget/2025-26/receipts.json — Revenue breakdown by category
- /data/budget/2025-26/expenditure.json — Ministry-wise expenditure with sub-schemes
- /data/budget/2025-26/sankey.json — Revenue-to-expenditure flow data
- /data/budget/2025-26/statewise.json — State-wise budget allocation
- /data/budget/2025-26/schemes.json — Major government schemes
- /data/budget/2025-26/treemap.json — Hierarchical expenditure data
- /data/budget/2025-26/trends.json — 20-year historical budget trends (FY 2005-06 to 2025-26): expenditure, receipts, fiscal deficit, fiscal deficit % GDP, revenue deficit % GDP
- /data/budget/2025-26/budget-vs-actual.json — Ministry-level Budget Estimate vs Revised Estimate vs Actual for 10 top ministries across 7 fiscal years (FY 2019-20 to 2025-26)
- /data/tax-calculator/slabs.json — Income tax slab rates (Old and New regime)
- /data/tax-calculator/expenditure-shares.json — Ministry spending shares for tax allocation

### Economy
- /data/economy/2025-26/summary.json — Economic headline numbers
- /data/economy/2025-26/gdp-growth.json — GDP growth time series
- /data/economy/2025-26/inflation.json — CPI and WPI inflation data + CPI by COICOP division (Food, Housing, Health, Transport, Education)
- /data/economy/2025-26/fiscal.json — Fiscal deficit and government finances
- /data/economy/2025-26/external.json — Trade balance, exports, imports
- /data/economy/2025-26/sectors.json — Sectoral GDP composition
- /data/economy/2025-26/indicators.json — Key economic indicators

### RBI
- /data/rbi/2025-26/summary.json — RBI headline numbers (repo rate, stance, forex, M3)
- /data/rbi/2025-26/monetary-policy.json — Repo rate decision history, CRR time series
- /data/rbi/2025-26/liquidity.json — Broad money growth and M3 % GDP
- /data/rbi/2025-26/credit.json — Domestic and private credit, lending/deposit rates
- /data/rbi/2025-26/forex.json — Forex reserves and INR/USD exchange rate
- /data/rbi/2025-26/indicators.json — All RBI indicators across 4 categories

### State Finances
- /data/states/2025-26/summary.json — State finances headline numbers
- /data/states/2025-26/gsdp.json — State-wise GSDP, growth rate, per capita + 3-year GSDP history for top 10 states
- /data/states/2025-26/revenue.json — Own tax revenue, central transfers, self-sufficiency ratio
- /data/states/2025-26/fiscal-health.json — Fiscal deficit and debt-to-GSDP by state
- /data/states/2025-26/indicators.json — All state indicators across 4 categories

### Census & Demographics
- /data/census/2025-26/summary.json — Census headline numbers (population, literacy, sex ratio)
- /data/census/2025-26/population.json — State-wise population, density, urban/rural split, decadal growth, national time series
- /data/census/2025-26/demographics.json — Age structure (young/working/elderly %), dependency ratio, vital stats (birth/death/fertility/life expectancy), urbanization trend
- /data/census/2025-26/literacy.json — State-wise literacy by gender, national time series
- /data/census/2025-26/health.json — IMR time series, state-level IMR, NFHS-5 state health (TFR, IMR, U5MR, stunting, wasting, immunization)
- /data/census/2025-26/indicators.json — All census indicators across 4 categories

### Education
- /data/education/2025-26/summary.json — Education headline numbers (total students, GER, PTR, spending)
- /data/education/2025-26/enrollment.json — National enrollment time series + state GER
- /data/education/2025-26/quality.json — Pupil-teacher ratios, learning outcomes, school infrastructure
- /data/education/2025-26/spending.json — Education expenditure trends (% GDP, % govt spending)
- /data/education/2025-26/indicators.json — All education indicators across 4 categories

### Employment
- /data/employment/2025-26/summary.json — Employment headline numbers (UR, LFPR, female LFPR)
- /data/employment/2025-26/unemployment.json — Unemployment time series (total/youth/gender) + state data
- /data/employment/2025-26/participation.json — LFPR time series (total/male/female) + state data
- /data/employment/2025-26/sectoral.json — Agriculture/industry/services employment shares
- /data/employment/2025-26/indicators.json — All employment indicators across 4 categories

### Healthcare
- /data/healthcare/2025-26/summary.json — Healthcare headline numbers (beds, doctors, spending, OOP)
- /data/healthcare/2025-26/infrastructure.json — Hospital beds, physicians, nurses time series + state data
- /data/healthcare/2025-26/spending.json — Health expenditure trends (% GDP, per capita, OOP)
- /data/healthcare/2025-26/disease.json — TB incidence, immunization coverage trends
- /data/healthcare/2025-26/indicators.json — All healthcare indicators across 4 categories

### Elections
- /data/elections/2025-26/summary.json — Elections headline numbers (turnout, electors, BJP/INC seats, women MPs, criminal cases %)
- /data/elections/2025-26/turnout.json — National turnout trend 1957-2024 (17 elections) + event annotations + state-wise 2024 breakdown
- /data/elections/2025-26/results.json — Party-wise seat evolution across 17 elections (stacked area format) + Lok Sabha 2024 party results + alliance totals
- /data/elections/2025-26/candidates.json — ADR data: criminal cases breakdown, assets distribution, education levels, top 20 wealthiest MPs, top 15 MPs by criminal cases
- /data/elections/2025-26/representation.json — Women MPs trend 1957-2024 with 33% reservation target
- /data/elections/2025-26/indicators.json — State-level election indicators
- /data/elections/2025-26/glossary.json — 15 election terms with plain-language definitions

### Crime
- /data/crime/2025-26/summary.json — Crime headline numbers (total crimes, crime rate, conviction rate, road deaths, cybercrime)
- /data/crime/2025-26/overview.json — National crime trend 2014-2022, IPC composition, state crime rates
- /data/crime/2025-26/women-safety.json — Crimes against women trend, crime types, state rates
- /data/crime/2025-26/road-accidents.json — Road accident trend, causes, state fatality rates
- /data/crime/2025-26/cybercrime.json — NCRB FIR trend, I4C complaints, crime types
- /data/crime/2025-26/police.json — Police ratios, state-wise data, women in police, vacancies
- /data/crime/2025-26/justice.json — Justice funnel (FIR to conviction), conviction rate, trial duration
- /data/crime/2025-26/indicators.json — State-level crime indicators across 5 categories
- /data/crime/2025-26/glossary.json — 15 crime terms with plain-language definitions

### Citizen Questions
- /data/questions.json — 161 curated citizen questions across 11 domains with answers and section anchors. Used by Cmd+K search.

### EMI Calculator
- /data/emi/loan-spreads.json — Curated loan spreads over repo rate for home (2.75%), car (3.5%), personal (8%) loans. Sources: SBI EBLR, HDFC, ICICI published rate cards.

## Key Takeaways & Question-First Search (Phase 9)

### Key Takeaways
Every story page has a "Key Takeaways" strip between the hero section and the first scrollytelling section. Four stat pills per domain, computed at runtime from source data. Each pill shows a headline number with a citizen-friendly label and scrolls to the relevant section on click.

Examples:
- Budget: "₹96/day — What govt. spends on you, daily", "31 paise — Of every rupee spent, borrowed"
- Economy: "6.5% — How fast the economy grew", "4.0% — How much prices rose (inflation)"
- RBI: "5.25% — The rate that decides your EMI", "$643B — India's forex safety net"
- States: "₹1.72L — Average state income per person", "36 — States and UTs, 36 different economies"
- Census: "943 — Girls per 1,000 boys, 57 are 'missing'", "1 in 4 — Indians who can't read or write"
- Education: "24.8 Cr — Students, more than Brazil's population", "26:1 — Students per teacher"
- Employment: "~13% — Youth unemployment, 3× the national rate", "~35% — Only 1 in 3 women in workforce"
- Healthcare: "~48% — You pay half your medical bills from your pocket", "0.5 beds — Per 1,000 (WHO says 3.5)"

### Question-First Search
The Cmd+K search overlay indexes 161 curated citizen questions across all 11 domains. Questions are phrased in natural language ("Why are prices rising?", "How much does the government spend on me per day?") and map to specific story page sections with one-sentence answers.

Question results appear with an amber QUESTION badge. Selecting a question navigates directly to the relevant section (e.g., "Why are prices rising?" → /economy#inflation).

## Chart sharing & embedding (Phase 7)

Every chart on the portal supports sharing and embedding:

### Chart actions overlay
Hover over any chart on desktop (or tap the share icon on mobile) to access:
- **PNG download** — Chart exported as an image with title, source attribution, and branding watermark
- **CSV download** — Underlying data exported as a comma-separated file
- **Permalink** — Direct URL with hash anchor to the specific chart section
- **Embed code** — Ready-to-use iframe snippet for external websites

### Embed routes
Any chart can be embedded via iframe using the pattern:
```
/embed/{domain}/{sectionId}
```

Available embed URLs (examples):
- /embed/budget/revenue — Revenue & Receipts waffle chart
- /embed/budget/flow — Revenue to Expenditure Sankey diagram
- /embed/economy/growth — Real GDP Growth line chart
- /embed/economy/inflation — CPI Inflation tracking
- /embed/rbi/monetary-policy — Repo Rate & Policy History
- /embed/rbi/forex — Forex Reserves chart
- /embed/states/gsdp — State GSDP Rankings
- /embed/census/population — Population & Growth
- /embed/education/enrollment — Gross Enrollment Ratios
- /embed/employment/participation — Labour Force Participation
- /embed/healthcare/infrastructure — Healthcare Infrastructure
- /embed/elections/turnout — Voter Turnout Trends
- /embed/crime/overview — Crime Overview (national trend, IPC composition)

Embed pages render with minimal chrome (no header/footer), responsive to container width, dark background, source attribution, and a link back to the full portal.

### Deep linking
- Scrollytelling sections have hash anchors: `/economy#growth`, `/rbi#forex`, etc.
- Explorer pages support URL query params: `/economy/explore?category=growth&indicator=gdp-growth`
- Both formats are shareable and bookmarkable.

### WhatsApp sharing
Mobile users get a "Share on WhatsApp" option that generates a one-stat image card (1200x630, <100KB) with the chart's hero statistic, source attribution, and a deep link.

## Tax calculator details

Supports both Old and New tax regimes for AY 2026-27 (FY 2025-26):
- Standard deduction: Rs 75,000 (New) / Rs 50,000 (Old)
- Old Regime deductions: Section 80C (Rs 1.5L cap), 80D health insurance (Rs 25K self + Rs 25K parents), 80CCD(1B) NPS (Rs 50K), Section 24(b) home loan interest (Rs 2L), HRA exemption, 80TTA savings interest (Rs 10K)
- Quick presets: "Salaried basics", "With home loan", "Max deductions"
- Section 87A rebate applied
- Surcharge slabs for high incomes
- 4% Health and Education Cess
